description: Bud Light sent personalized cans to trans influencer Dylan Mulvaney in April 2023, triggering a massive conservative boycott. Brand favorability among Republicans crashed ~40 points. Sales dropped 26% YoY. Tests culture war brand polarization and boycott cascade dynamics.

key_events.description: Dylan Mulvaney posts Instagram video with personalized Bud Light can on April 1. Initially low-key, standard influencer marketing.
Kid Rock posts video shooting Bud Light cases with assault rifle. Conservative boycott goes viral on social media.
Anheuser-Busch CEO issues statement but avoids directly addressing the partnership. Both sides feel alienated. Sales crater 26% YoY.
Two Bud Light marketing executives placed on leave. Brand loses #1 US beer position to Modelo Especial.
Bud Light pivots messaging to patriotic/sports themes. Conservative boycott persists. Progressive consumers also boycott over perceived abandonment of Mulvaney.

calibration_notes: Tests asymmetric polarization cascade. Key patterns: pre-existing brand had broad moderate appeal, culture war framing created rapid partisan sorting, corporate non-response amplified backlash from both sides, boycott stickiness (sales never recovered to baseline), cross-partisan alienation (both conservatives and progressives turned against brand). Morning Consult favorability data used for trajectory.
